tweets.text: Thank you, Mr. Secretary. https://t.co/32JfAj911g

In the week at State, @POTUS visits the Department and addresses the nation on the Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage deal. @StateDeptSpox holds a final press briefing, @SecBlinken delivers remarks at the @AtlanticCouncil, and addresses reporters from the press briefing room. https://t.co/hVpNEM3DDP

Tomorrow we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and remember his devotion to advancing equality, social justice, and economic opportunity for all. His vision and words continue to leave an impact on communities around the world. https://t.co/qAY3qY5LqD

The U.S. responsibly manages its strategic competition with the People’s Republic of China. We collaborate on shared concerns, work to reduce misunderstandings, and keep lines of communication open. A new office here at State focuses exclusively on this important relationship. https://t.co/8oU53LMYrf

Security, stability, and prosperity in today’s digital world means driving innovation and harnessing technology for the benefit of humanity — while minimizing the risks of critical and emerging technologies. https://t.co/mZeZ9xOvW3

The U.S. leads in tackling the climate crisis. We've invested heavily in clean energy, led the world to mobilize climate finance for vulnerable communities, and focused global attention on the urgent need to cut super pollutants like methane. https://t.co/g2jhPDhEdq

The U.S. has renewed and deepened our alliances and partnerships over the past four years — and we forged new ones. 🌍🤝 Working together, we can address the toughest shared challenges of our time. https://t.co/GXE56MyJJk

As historic numbers of people flee their homes around the world, the U.S. brings a humane and effective approach to humanitarian crises. We’re forging partnerships around the world to address the crisis, while supporting refugees in the U.S. through programs like @WelcomeCorps. https://t.co/FFWUt4WPwP

Stronger democracies mean deeper partnerships, more peaceful societies, and more dependable markets. Over the past four years, we’ve demonstrated that U.S. leadership on democratic renewal is vital in a rapidly interconnected and changing world. https://t.co/V7ed7GmVKl

In an interview with @CBSSunday, @SecBlinken  discussed our work over the past four years to re-engage with the world and to rejuvenate, re-energize, and reimagine our alliances and partnerships. https://t.co/dCOpLbUQ0T

.@SecBlinken takes a look back at the time he traveled to the Africa Cup of Nations in Côte d’Ivoire. 

As the Secretary says, sports — one of our best diplomatic tools — bring people together. https://t.co/DkHJNb8tGI

Another path is possible. We’ve seen it. A path toward greater integration, greater opportunity – toward peace, with lasting security. - @SecBlinken on future of the Middle East https://t.co/oILacs2NFn

You’re working every day to make things just a little bit better, a little bit more peaceful, a little bit more full of hope, of opportunity. That’s your mission, and you do it so well. - @SecBlinken’s final message to State Department workforce today https://t.co/zNmTTNV03z
Listen to @SecBlinken’s conversation with David Remnick of the @NewYorker’s Radio Hour. https://t.co/Y33YO7vD7j

The U.S. is helping build a more stable, inclusive global economy. In four years, we’ve strengthened supply chains, made historic investments in global infrastructure, and forged new regional partnerships that make our economies more resilient. https://t.co/4C7RPJUCAG

The U.S. is strengthening global health. We donated nearly 700M COVID-19 vaccine doses to 117 countries and economies, supported 20.6M people with HIV treatments this year, and pledged up to 1M mpox vaccine doses. We also created a new bureau focused on health security. https://t.co/QUOoOxXYqo

Secretary Blinken reflects on his visit to the Abrahamic Family House in Abu Dhabi. 

This place in the United Arab Emirates brings together a church, a mosque, and a synagogue as a symbol of unity among the Abrahamic faiths. https://t.co/oYhxFpBRYQ

Following his final trip as Secretary of State, @SecBlinken discussed the United States’ role on the world stage, including supporting Ukraine and reinvigorating @NATO, in an interview with @CBSSunday. https://t.co/DHa6R0WHI4
.@SecBlinken looks back on four years of revitalizing alliances and advancing shared values in an increasingly complex world, following his final trip as Secretary of State. https://t.co/fFh2Qv4AjT
.@SecBlinken delivers farewell remarks to Department personnel. https://t.co/7kxxpV5eOo https://t.co/Uivon3Figh
When @SecBlinken started as Secretary of State four years ago, he pledged to fulfill eight goals. Four years later we look back on results delivered. https://t.co/u6hqYOnJ4v
We've worked to modernize our diplomacy so that we're more agile, we're more effective, we're more prepared for the challenges of a new era. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/MHyqyVQ5XL
Thanks to historic investments at home and around the world, the United States can now operate from a position of greater strength. —@SecBlinken https://t.co/nMkUYKefh4

Diplomacy isn't just shaped by governments, it's driven by people. Through our @ECAatState people-to-people programs, we have engaged and empowered people and communities around the world, improving foreign relations and reinforcing peace and security here at home.
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks to the press from the State Department. https://t.co/2bNfp5dByt https://t.co/AKmPy6p9Bd
.@POTUS outlines the ceasefire and hostage deal reached today between Israel and Hamas, brokered through the diplomatic efforts of the United States, Egypt, and Qatar. https://t.co/vi41E5rm9x

Israelis must decide what relationship they want with Palestinians. That cannot be the illusion that Palestinians will accept being a non-people without national rights. Seven million Israeli Jews and some five million Palestinians are rooted in the same land. Neither is going anywhere. - @SecBlinken

To avoid misunderstanding, we found ways to work with each other to address climate change, to reduce the flow of fentanyl in the United States. - @POTUS on U.S. relations with the People’s Republic of China https://t.co/N8xbyuUQOe

The Biden administration's primary goal in the Middle East was not to repeat the blunder of years past of trying to transform its governments or its societies, but rather to transform relations with, between, and among U.S. partners in the region. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/whxz0wnAlr
A picture is worth a thousand words. @SecBlinken sat down with our team to reflect on key moments in American diplomacy during his tenure as the 71st U.S. Secretary of State. https://t.co/8l8dJsnDXR
America is stronger, our alliances are stronger. - @POTUS https://t.co/nIyG1lCogK

As @SecBlinken traveled around the world, he met extraordinary people from all walks of life. People who are working to make the world a better place. The Secretary reconnected virtually with some of these inspiring people. https://t.co/eDW1yHRnoT
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks on the Middle East at the @AtlanticCouncil in Washington, D.C. https://t.co/hS8vzoGyce https://t.co/nsr8WyzdNO
.@SecBlinken sat down with @CBSSunday to discuss the impact and importance of U.S. engagement around the world. Watch the full interview: https://t.co/iJQEcDfWqp

Today, @POTUS spoke about U.S. foreign policy from the State Department. After four years of the Biden-Harris administration, America has more friends and stronger alliances — our adversaries are weaker and under pressure. https://t.co/WFdOvVAHCR
.@POTUS delivers a foreign policy address at the State Department on his Administration's work to strengthen America and lead the world. https://t.co/xWkkM6ycN5  https://t.co/EV8pJN5NZM

.@DeputySecState sits down with chef @DannyLeeDC, Special Envoy for North Korean Human Rights Issues Ambassador Julie Turner, and a North Korean defector for a dinner table conversation on how Korean cuisine keeps traditions, memories, and heritage alive. https://t.co/EsuW3p066S

This week at State, @SecBlinken kicks off his 21st Indo-Pacific trip, reaffirming the U.S.-ROK alliance and strengthening our ties with Japan. He meets with European officials in France & Rome, and receives the Légion d’honneur. Finally, we honor President Jimmy Carter’s legacy. https://t.co/HIXnHfxU4S
While in Paris, French President @EmmanuelMacron awarded @SecBlinken the prestigious Légion d’honneur at the Élysée Palace. https://t.co/Vf0DJ9EKhO

The cooperation between Japan and the United States is at the heart of critical partnerships and networks that are tackling issues that are making a big difference in the lives of our citizens. https://t.co/h6h1rLASNZ

Our countries will continue to work together and to lead together on the world stage – not out of convenience, not out of charity, but because the challenges we face demand our engagement, demand our partnership, demand our cooperation. - @SecBlinken in Paris https://t.co/WQobOtifyK

The United States and France have a shared determination to advance our shared interests and values. Our partnership is essential as we work together to build a world that’s safer, more secure, and more prosperous. https://t.co/zN2kr0dP4b
Here’s a look back at Jimmy Carter’s remarkable life in photos: https://t.co/JUJGznZ4CD
Watch the State Funeral in celebration of and thanksgiving for the life of former President James Earl Carter Jr., at Washington National Cathedral https://t.co/sDLFDZR5Zy
I have one life and one chance to make it count for something. — Jimmy Carter, 39th President of the United States https://t.co/aHxJhjctbB

Members of the Washington diplomatic corps were invited to sign a condolence book to pay tribute to former President Jimmy Carter at the State Department last week. Condolence books were also available at many U.S. embassies and consulates for mourners to pay their respects. https://t.co/n88YDLe5Ll
As we look at the last four years we’ve seen an alliance, a partnership, a friendship that’s grown stronger than it’s ever been before. – @SecBlinken in Tokyo 🇺🇸 🇯🇵 https://t.co/XLCrpbwLIy

Four years ago, @SecBlinken’s first overseas trip as Secretary of State was to Japan.

While in Tokyo this week for his seventh trip to Japan, he lauded the work Japan and the U.S. have done together to make a difference in the lives of our people and people around the world. https://t.co/ZhiKfVdClg

The U.S.-France friendship is vital to international peace, prosperity, and security. France is our NATO Ally and a key partner on every issue of global significance.

@SecBlinken travels to Paris today to collaborate on solutions to challenges. https://t.co/AnkGKEmRcl
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with French Foreign Minister Barrot, in Paris. https://t.co/vfu6TeKJYU https://t.co/moyxTvopmf

The State Department honors the life, service, and legacy of former President Carter. He was the 39th U.S. President, a Navy veteran, a Nobel Peace Prize Laureate, and a humanitarian who dedicated his life to promoting peace, democracy, and human rights. https://t.co/XrYXyunKhE

For seven decades, the United States-Republic of Korea Alliance has been the linchpin of peace, security, prosperity in Northeast Asia, the Indo-Pacific, but also increasingly around the world. https://t.co/tlfHWUdHv7

The Republic of Korea has been an extraordinary partner for the U.S. over many decades, but in particular during these last four years. We’ve seen that in the work that we’re doing together not only bilaterally between our countries, but increasingly, globally. – @SecBlinken https://t.co/oLkJr64oOY

The U.S.-Japan Alliance is now truly a global partnership, where we stand shoulder to shoulder on the key challenges of our time.

@SecBlinken was in Tokyo today to further strengthen our partnership — and to tackle today’s greatest challenges. https://t.co/7TSapK0vin
Merry Christmas and Happy New Year to all who are celebrating Orthodox Christmas today. https://t.co/wyWdwqN3lw
During travel to Seoul, @SecBlinken held a joint press availability with Republic of Korea Foreign Minister Cho Tae-yul. https://t.co/2xLVNphGN4

Whether helping Ukraine sustain its economy amid Russia’s brutal war or ensuring food security for people in dire circumstances, the U.S. is committed to helping those in need. @PowerUSAID discusses the impact of U.S. humanitarian assistance: https://t.co/aUfguYAIHG

January is Human Trafficking Prevention Month and this year marks the 25th anniversary of the Trafficking Victims Protection Act. We continue to raise awareness, stand with survivors, and take action to prevent this violation of human rights. https://t.co/ftxau3AwFP

Traffickers are using tech to recruit, defraud, groom, and exploit victims around the world. Read the 2024 #TIPReport to learn how advocates are leveraging technologies to prevent crime, protect victims, and prosecute traffickers. #EndHumanTrafficking https://t.co/lpfKJBQcwB https://t.co/wuftt3S1Qu

The United States, Italy, and the European Union are investing in a trade route that connects Zambia and the Democratic Republic of the Congo to Angola and beyond through Angola’s Atlantic Port of Lobito. Learn more about the revitalized corridor:
Working together as the Quad, Australia, India, Japan, and the U.S. are delivering positive outcomes for the people of our region. We're stronger when we work together. https://t.co/KVmmv8VDXU

Twenty years ago, in response to the 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami, Australia, India, Japan, and the United States came together as the grouping now known as the Quad. What began as an emergency response to a catastrophe has grown into a full-fledged partnership. https://t.co/7sWazLzkzd
Happy 2025! We wish all a happy, healthy, and prosperous new year. https://t.co/wOCw22k4Fm
